---
title: Project Proposal
author:
- Collin Farrell
- Paarth Mahajan
- Aayush Sharma
- Tony Zheng
format:
    html:
        embed-resources: true
---

## Project Title

## Research Questions
### (RQ.1)
Does the feels-like temperature of a day affect the # of rentals after accounting for other weather conditions (e.g. precipitation, humidity, and windspeed)?

### (RQ.2)
Do weather conditions affect registered vs. casual users differently?

## Model

The response variable of our model will be the number of rentals each hour. Denoting the number of bike rentals at hour $i$ to be $Y_i$ (or `cnt` in the dataset), we assume
$$
Y_i \sim \text{Poisson}(\lambda_i) \\
$$

with link function
$$
\log(\lambda_i) = X\beta
$$

for $X \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times (p+1)}$ and $\beta \in \mathbb{R}^{p + 1}$, where $n$ is the number of samples in the hour dataset, and $p$ is the number of predictors we're including, or the number of rows in the [Predictors](#predictors) section.

## Predictors

Our model will include:

Predictor    | Reason
-------------|-----------------------------------------------------
`temp` | To control for the effect of temperature on the number of bike rentals
`windspeed` 
`weathersit` | To examine the effect of the amount of precipitation
